reconciliation.scope_note: lifecycle counts are cumulative since canonical accounting began; baseline counts are legacy ledger totals that predate it; ledger/gate totals == baseline + accounted.

learning.note: the bot's own settled-trade experience (calibrated edge model) adjusts the directional probability; it grows as more trades settle. The execution-quality gate, paper-realism, and reconciliation are unchanged and still veto every trade â learning can never bypass them.

last_note.summary: With n=53 settled trades, overall edge is modestly positive (wr 0.604, ev_after_cost 0.116) but driven by a handful of regimes; DOWN_STRONG, trending/Hurst, normal BB, strong ADX, sell-pressure CVD, bearish MTF alignment, and 120-240s TTC show confirmed positive EV with n>=8 and wr well above breakeven. Most other buckets are either n<8 noise or show flat/negative realized pnl despite positive ev. Sample remains too small for high-confidence live deployment.
failing: active volume state (n=34, wr=0.559, negative pnl)
expansion/squeeze BB states (combined n=37, wr<=0.5, negative pnl)
UP direction overall (n=24, wr=0.542, negative realized pnl)
warnings: total n=53 still low; many buckets n<8 must be ignored
positive ev but some high-wr buckets have small realized pnl
observe-only mode means no execution slippage captured
focus_next: accumulate more samples in normal BB + strong ADX + sell_pressure CVD intersection
monitor whether DOWN_STRONG edge persists above 0.65 wr with n>40

history.summary: With 53 settled trades, DOWN_STRONG signals in trending regimes (Hurst, ADX strong_trend, bearish HTF/MTF alignment, sell_pressure CVD, bb normal, range_middle, ttc 120-240s, dead volume) show confirmed positive EV (win-rates 0.64-0.83, n>=12-50) while UP signals and long holds remain negative. Sample size still limits calibration on most sub-buckets; overall EV after cost is modestly positive but driven by a narrow set of conditions. No material change possible as this is the first analysis.

With n=53 total settled trades, overall win-rate 60.4% and positive EV after costs (0.116) is driven by a narrow set of regimes; DOWN_STRONG (n=26, WR 69%), trending/Hurst (n=50), normal BB state (n=16, WR 87.5%), bearish-aligned MTF (n=12, WR 83%), sell-pressure CVD (n=19, WR 74%), and 120-240s TTC (n=26, WR 73%) all clear n>=8, positive EV, and WR comfortably above breakeven. UP direction, active volume, squeeze/expansion BB, and >=240s TTC show negative realized PnL and are likely noise. Sample remains too small for robust multi-way interactions.

With 53 settled trades the bot shows modest positive EV (overall wr 0.604, avg_ev 0.116) driven by a handful of buckets that meet n>=8, wr comfortably above breakeven and positive realized pnl; DOWN_STRONG, trending/Hurst, BB-normal, bearish_mtf_aligned, sell_pressure/CVD and 120-240s TTC stand out as confirmed. Most other slices remain too small or show wr <=0.55 with negative pnl and are still noise. No prior analysis exists so all observations are new.

With only 53 settled trades, DOWN_STRONG signals in trending regimes (esp. 120-240s TTC, normal BB state, bearish-aligned MTF, sell-pressure CVD, strong ADX) show confirmed positive EV and win-rates well above breakeven after costs; UP signals and most expansions/squeezes remain marginal or negative. Many buckets still fall below the n>=8 threshold and are ignored as noise. Sample remains too small for robust conclusions.
changes_since_last: No prior_analysis supplied; baseline established from first 53 trades

history.summary: With 53 settled trades, DOWN_STRONG signals in trending regimes, 120-240s TTC, normal BB state, sell_pressure CVD, and bearish_aligned MTF show confirmed positive EV (0.11-0.14) and win-rates 0.65-0.83 above breakeven with n>=12; overall sample remains small and selective (only 53/39189 accepted). UP_STRONG and long-TTC (>=240s) buckets are failing with negative realized PnL despite marginal EV. No material changes possible on first analysis; focus must stay strictly observe-only.

With 53 settled trades (n>=8 filter applied), DOWN_STRONG signals in trending regimes, normal BB state, 120-240s TTC, z-score -2..1, sell_pressure CVD, bearish MTF alignment, and strong ADX show confirmed positive EV (wr 0.64-0.83, avg_ev 0.11-0.14 after costs). UP signals, >=240s TTC, expansion/squeeze BB, and active volume buckets are weaker or breakeven-negative. Sample remains small and observe-only; no material change possible without prior_analysis.
changes_since_last: first analysis; no prior baseline available

history.summary: With n=53 total settled trades, overall win-rate 60.4% and positive avg EV after cost (0.116) confirm mild edge, driven almost entirely by DOWN_STRONG signals in trending regimes. Buckets with n>=8 show confirmed profitability in DOWN direction, bearish-aligned MTF, normal BB state, 120-240s TTC, strong ADX, sell-pressure CVD and range-middle; UP signals and >=240s TTC remain negative or marginal. Most other slices have n<8 and are ignored as unreliable.

With n=53 settled trades, overall edge is modestly positive (wr 0.604, ev_after_cost 0.116) but driven by a handful of regimes; DOWN_STRONG, trending/Hurst, normal BB, strong ADX, sell-pressure CVD, bearish MTF alignment, and 120-240s TTC show confirmed positive EV with n>=8 and wr well above breakeven. Most other buckets are either n<8 noise or show flat/negative realized pnl despite positive ev. Sample remains too small for high-confidence live deployment.

analyst_A.note: observe-only Grok research analyst that LEARNS the bot's trading patterns from its growing settled-trade/selectivity/late-window evidence and refines across cycles (continuity). Diagnostics only â never trades.

predictor_B.note: observe-only Grok P(up) per signal; graded vs realized BTC move before it could ever be trusted; never places/sizes/bypasses a trade.

grok_signal_intel.note: A analyzes signal-learning performance; B predicts P(up) per signal and is graded vs realized moves. Both observe-only â never place/size/bypass a trade; the execution gate remains the sole trade authority.

aggression.note: loosens (more explore/looser exploit/larger size) as acted trades profit; tightens on losses; circuit breaker is the hard floor.

grok_decider.note: Grok decides; bot executes (paper). shadow=decide+grade only (no trade), follow=engine follows direction/size subject to the deterministic floor (execution realism, risk caps, freshness). Fail-closed -> no_trade.

headlines: Bitcoin trading near $60K with ongoing ETF outflows (~$444M daily net as of Jun 26)
No major macro prints, liquidations, or regulatory headlines in last ~30 minutes

verifier.note: independent Claude maker-checker; can only veto/shrink a Grok-proposed paper trade, never force one; fail-open; PAPER ONLY.

last_note.summary: 109 settled trades, 61.5% win rate, +$12 PnL, but profit factor 1.06 and avg loss ($4.64) > avg win ($3.09) indicate fragile edge. Only 6 trades in exploitable contexts (high edge_quality, >=240s TTC, chop_noise markov) show 66.7% WR / +$9.32. zscore=-1..1 and regime=noise are losing (0% WR, -$15 combined). Sample sizes too small for live.

knob_recommendations.reason: >=240s TTC cluster: 6 trades, 66.7% WR, +$9.32; shorter TTC underperforms
edge_quality=high subset profitable; raise floor to filter marginal signals
spread<=0.01 bucket shows edge; wider spreads erode alpha via slippage
down WR 64% vs up 50%; modest asymmetry warrants slight sizing tilt

new_lessons.rule: zscore=-1..1: 0% WR, -$10; mean-reversion absent in noise â block trades lacking divergence signal
hurst_regime=noise: 1 trade, 0% WR, -$5; randomness dominates â require trending or insufficient_data (2/2 wins)
ttc>=240s: 66.7% WR vs <240s poor; longer resolution windows allow signal to express vs noise decay
170/177 exec rejections = underdog_price_below_floor; tight floor protects but may hide mispriced tailsâmonitor
PF 1.06, avg_loss > avg_win; edge exists but RR poor â prioritize high-conviction over volume until PF>1.3

research_loop.note: strategy-layer research meta-loop: independent model proposes exploit/avoid contexts + bounded knob nudges + lessons. Observe-only unless auto_apply (whitelisted+bounded). PAPER ONLY.

recent.rule: profit_factor=1.046 with avg_loss=$4.63 > avg_win=$3.06; one bad streak flips you negativeâno live until PF>1.2
max_drawdown=$43.62 vs net_pnl=$8.82 (5:1 risk/reward realized); unacceptable for liveâneed 3:1 or better
n=106 settled, but buckets have n=1-3; you need 500+ trades and >=30 per slice before claiming bucket-level edge
if execution_gate rejects >50% for underdog_price_below_floor, market data is stale or floor miscalibratedâinvestigate data feed before any floor adjustment
do not trade contexts with n<30; current max n=4 means all slice-level statistics are pure noise
avg_loss $4.64 vs avg_win $3.06 is fatal; require avg_win >= 1.5x avg_loss before claiming any edge
profit_factor 1.02 is breakeven after real costs; need >=1.5 sustained over >=200 trades to consider live readiness

tradingview.stop_condition: ingest only â never trade authority

loops.note: formalized sub-loops (data/signal/verify/execute/risk/research/news/lessons); each has a trigger + skill + verifier + verifiable stop + a liveness watchdog (last_beat_age_s/stalled). PAPER ONLY.

arbitrage.note: arb halt only on anomaly (guaranteed_profit<=0); risk-free P&L is segregated

dependency_arbitrage.note: Lane B halt on booking anomaly only; walk-forward is observability

stop_conditions.note: quantitative kill switches checked each tick from settled ledger evidence; NOT the LLM's opinion. Halting blocks NEW entries only.

promotion.note: observe-only diagnostic; eligible buckets are NOT auto-promoted to trading authority unless promotion_allowed_by_config is true AND explicitly wired. The execution gate remains the sole trade authority.

cex_lead_edge.promotion_rule: n>=min AND wilson_lower(win_rate)>breakeven AND Brier_cex<Brier_market AND avg_pnl>0
cex_lead_edge.note: CEX-lead latency edge; benchmark is the MARKET price (already ~0.21 Brier). SHADOW grades only; GATED can PROPOSE a side on a proven bucket but the execution-quality gate + safety floor remain authoritative. PAPER ONLY.

arbitrage.note: risk-free dutch book: BUY-both (asks<$1) + SELL-both (mint $1 set, sell bids>$1); deterministic P&L; NEVER blended into directional. PAPER ONLY.

last_violations.description: 15m up-mid below nested 5m up-mid: P(up_15m) cannot be < P(up_5m) for overlapping window

dependency_arbitrage.note: LCMM nested-window scanner + optional paper execution on validated nested_implication violations.

arb_graph.note: Graph is rebuilt each tick from open windows; Grok edges are never tradable alone.

accepted_dependencies.description: Both 5m windows (640302, 640311) are strictly contained inside the 15m window 640304 (timestamps 1782639000-1782639900).

baseline_cohort_gate.note: baseline quant path: 180-240s TTC band (scaled on 15m), high edge + strong CEX; UP blocked until promoted; green_path=15m DOWN cohort only (TV observe-only)

learned_selectivity_gate.note: rejects ONLY buckets confidently below their own breakeven win-rate (Wilson upper bound < breakeven AND net-negative PnL); coin-flip / not-significant buckets are NOT rejected. Can only make the bot MORE selective â never trades, resizes, or bypasses the execution gate. Exploration tracked separately.

bucket_evidence.rule: blocked iff confidently_losing AND fdr_significant (Wilson upper < breakeven, PF < min, BH-FDR q=0.10)

counterfactual.note: in-sample replay using final accumulated bucket evidence (diagnostic estimate)

gate.note: when enabled, only late-window AND high-conviction setups may trade; can only PREVENT trades, never force/size/bypass the execution gate.

edge_measurement.note: observe-only: grades whether late-window high-conviction trades win more (cohort vs other) from live settled trades; never affects trading.

tradingview.note: TradingView alerts are candidate signals only â they cannot place, resize, or bypass trades; the strategy + execution gate remain the sole trade authority.

edge_vs_5min_outcome.note: observe-only: did the TradingView signal at entry predict the 5-min Chainlink outcome (signal_hit_rate vs baseline_up_rate), and did aligning help the bot win (aligned vs opposed bot_win_rate)? Never affects trading.

rsi_trend.note: observe-only: learns P(next 5-min outcome | RSI alert trend state) from the alert history and scores its own next-window predictions. Never trades.

promotion.note: observe-only diagnostic; eligible buckets are NOT auto-promoted to trading authority unless promotion_allowed_by_config is true AND explicitly wired. The execution gate remains the sole trade authority.

signal_gate.note: when active, a paper trade is taken only if a fresh TradingView signal agrees with the side; it can only PREVENT trades, never force or bypass them.

context_gate.note: hard prior gate: blocks proven-losing entry contexts (volume spikes, noise regime, far-from-resolution) before enough samples exist for the learned selectivity gate. Can only PREVENT trades â never forces, sizes, or bypasses the execution gate, which remains the sole trade authority.

down_bias_gate.note: restrict-only: harvest DOWN/TV asymmetry by blocking proven-losing UP stacks

mtf_gate.note: observe-only conflict veto: blocks 4m/5m fast-pair conflict only (loop arch default). require_confirm/side_align are opt-in restrict-only layers, not trade authority.

confidence_tier.note: At 15m TTC sweet spot, TV MTF regime adjusts min_edge/max_price only. TV trade gates remain off per operator lock.
